# FQHC Talent

> California's FQHC Strategic Intelligence Platform — executive dashboards, workforce data, policy tracking, compliance tools, OKR training, free career tools, and 220+ FQHC profiles for leaders and professionals navigating the 2026 community health crisis.

FQHC Talent (https://www.fqhctalent.com) is a strategic intelligence platform for Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) in California. It serves two audiences: (1) FQHC executives and HR directors tracking the political, financial, and workforce landscape; and (2) community health professionals seeking career tools, salary data, and job listings.

The site covers: H.R. 1 Medicaid cuts, CalAIM programs (ECM, Community Supports), FQHC funding policy, the California workforce crisis, AI adoption in community health, FQHC compliance (HRSA, HIPAA, billing), and salary benchmarks across 9 CA regions. All insights cite primary sources (government, NACHC, CPCA, CHCF, KFF).

Key data assets: 214 California FQHCs with resilience scores, 1,634+ job listings across 30+ FQHCs, 139+ intelligence items across 9 categories, 28 AI adoption tracker items with 8 vendor comparisons, 25 OKR templates with Excel trackers, 22 case studies, 18 masterclass modules, 20 certifications, 13 operational guides, 20 layoff events tracking 3,477+ displaced workers, 62 salary benchmarks across 9 regions, 9 regional intelligence dashboards, 214 per-FQHC strategic reports, and 15 bilingual blog articles. Updated daily via automated intelligence pipeline.

## Intelligence & Monitoring

- [Intelligence Dashboard](https://www.fqhctalent.com/) — Breaking intel feed, funding cliff countdowns, workforce displacement data, compliance calendar, and undocumented access policy watch. Homepage serves as the executive intelligence dashboard.
- [AI Tracker](https://www.fqhctalent.com/ai-tracker) — 28 AI implementations tracked across FQHC sector: ambient documentation (Abridge, Sunoh, Nabla, Suki, DAX, athenaOne), RCM automation, clinical decision support, scheduling AI. 8 vendor comparisons with EHR compatibility matrix and FQHC fit scores.
- [Layoff Tracker](https://www.fqhctalent.com/layoffs) — 20 layoff events, 3,477+ displaced workers, WARN Act data for California FQHCs and health systems. UC Berkeley projects 72K-145K CA healthcare job losses from Medicaid cuts.
- [Blog](https://www.fqhctalent.com/blog) — 15 bilingual articles on FQHC careers, Medi-Cal policy, salary data, and ECM programs with primary source citations
- [Regional Intelligence](https://www.fqhctalent.com/intelligence/los-angeles) — 9 per-region dashboards: LA, San Diego, Bay Area, Sacramento, Central Valley, Inland Empire, Central Coast, North State, North Coast. Per-region FQHC tables, resilience distribution, top programs, EHR landscape, layoff impact, job counts.
- [Salary Intelligence](https://www.fqhctalent.com/salary-data) — P25/P50/P75 benchmarks for 62 FQHC roles across 9 California regions with regional cost-of-living adjustments and career progression charts

## Compliance Hub

- [Compliance Overview](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance) — FQHC regulatory compliance dashboard with downloadable tools (OSV checklist, compliance calendar, risk matrix)
- [HRSA Audits & OSV](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance/hrsa-audits) — 19 Operational Site Visit program requirements with CFR citations, preparation checklists, and estimated hours per requirement
- [HIPAA & Privacy](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance/hipaa) — Business Associate Agreements, breach notification protocol, annual security risk assessment, workforce training requirements
- [Billing & Fraud Prevention](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance/billing) — PPS same-day billing rules, documentation standards, coding accuracy (ICD-10/CPT), False Claims Act, 340B compliance, managed care contracts
- [Compliance Calendar](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance/calendar) — Month-by-month regulatory deadlines for HRSA, HIPAA, and billing with preparation timelines
- [Regulatory Knowledge Base](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compliance/knowledge-base) — Searchable database of FQHC regulations with CFR citations, practical implications, and primary source links

## OKR Learning & Strategy

- [OKR Individual Course](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/okr-course) — Free 6-module interactive OKR course (45 min) with exercises, scoring simulators, and AI-powered capstone critique. Bilingual (EN/ES).
- [Team OKR Sprint](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/okr-team-sprint) — Free 4-session async sprint (2 hrs total) for executive teams to build real OKRs together
- [OKR Templates](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/okrs) — 25 crisis change management OKR templates across 5 domains with downloadable Excel trackers (revenue diversification, workforce retention, quality & compliance, operational efficiency, growth & innovation)

## Strategy Resources

- [Executive Guides](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/guides) — 22 real FQHC case studies using Rumelt's Good Strategy framework (PureView, MCR Health, Highland Health, AltaMed, Sun River Health, and more)
- [Case Studies Index](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/case-studies) — Outcomes-focused index linking to full strategic analysis
- [Masterclass](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/masterclass) — 18 deep-dive modules for the 2026 crisis: financial survival, revenue recovery, undocumented care, fundraising, healthcare economics, leadership
- [Healthcare Economics](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/economics) — PPS, 340B, FMAP, HCC risk adjustment, CalAIM ECM, SB 525 explained at 3 levels (beginner, intermediate, advanced)
- [Execution Frameworks](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/frameworks) — 12 change management and decision frameworks adapted for FQHCs
- [Thought Leaders](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/leaders) — 28 FQHC influencers: NACHC leadership, state PCA directors, innovative CEOs, policy experts, AI pioneers
- [Funding Impact](https://www.fqhctalent.com/funding-impact) — H.R. 1 policy timeline, revenue strategies, enrollment strategies with interactive visualizations
- [Scope-of-Practice](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/scope-of-practice) — 10 CA FQHC roles, delegation matrix (47 tasks), CA BPC citations, supervision chains, revenue impact modeling
- [Cultural Humility](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/cultural-humility) — CLAS Standards, Tervalon & Murray-Garcia framework, 5 workforce diversity scenarios, 20 competencies across 6 domains
- [The Movement](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/movement) — FQHC movement history 1960-2026: 30 events, 5 eras, 8 cross-cultural alliances (Cesar Chavez, Black Panthers, Dr. Geiger, Asian Health Services)
- [Transition Toolkit](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/offboarding) — 3-tier workforce transition service for FQHCs facing layoffs (free tools, managed transition, placement partnership)
- [Resilience Scorecard](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/resilience) — 214 FQHCs scored across 5 weighted dimensions (program diversity 25%, workforce stability 20%, data maturity 15%, quality indicators 20%, financial positioning 20%). Grades A-F. Searchable, sortable, filterable.
- [Clinic Simulator](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/clinic-simulator) — Model staffing, scheduling, and revenue scenarios for your FQHC with 3 size presets (small/mid/large)
- [Revenue Simulator](https://www.fqhctalent.com/strategy/revenue-simulator) — What-if modeling for revenue optimization: payer mix, team composition, visit volume

## FQHC Directory & Profiles

- [California FQHC Directory](https://www.fqhctalent.com/directory) — 214 FQHCs with programs, EHR systems, Glassdoor ratings, resilience scores, salary data, and interactive map
- [FQHC Strategic Reports](https://www.fqhctalent.com/report/altamed) — 220 individual per-FQHC strategic intelligence reports with executive summary, resilience assessment, threat landscape, workforce intel, and action items
- [Compare FQHCs](https://www.fqhctalent.com/compare) — Side-by-side comparison of 2-3 FQHCs across resilience, Glassdoor ratings, programs, funding, union status, and workforce data

## Career Tools (100% Free, No Login Required)

- [Jobs](https://www.fqhctalent.com/jobs) — 1,634+ job listings across 30+ roles and 9 California regions, updated weekly
- [Resume Builder](https://www.fqhctalent.com/resume-builder) — Template-based resume builder with 8 FQHC-specific role templates (CHW, care coordinator, MA, RN, NP, PA, physician, dentist)
- [Career Assessment](https://www.fqhctalent.com/career-insights) — 5-domain behavioral assessment with role-specific questions, STARS type selector, and personalized 90-day onboarding plan
- [Interview Prep](https://www.fqhctalent.com/interview-prep) — Role-specific FQHC interview questions with STAR framework tips, red flags, and follow-ups for 10+ roles
- [Career Roadmap](https://www.fqhctalent.com/career-roadmap) — 5 career tracks (community health, clinical ops, behavioral health, revenue/admin, nursing), 4 levels each, CA salary data, 9 regional multipliers
- [Certifications](https://www.fqhctalent.com/certifications) — 20 CA-specific certifications with cost, duration, salary impact, issuing body, and where to obtain in California
- [Career Resources](https://www.fqhctalent.com/resources) — 18 free/low-cost programs: NHSC loan repayment ($75K), free training, professional development
- [Workplace Guides](https://www.fqhctalent.com/guides) — 13 operational guides: ECM workflows, revenue cycle, billing compliance, grant lifecycle, CalAIM, and more
- [Team Readiness](https://www.fqhctalent.com/team-readiness) — 5-domain leadership assessment for FQHC managers and directors with management actions and Liberating Structures
- [Priority Intake](https://www.fqhctalent.com/fast-track) — Displaced worker priority matching and free career tools

## Key Topics Covered

- H.R. 1 "One Big Beautiful Bill" — largest proposed Medicaid cuts in history, impact on California FQHCs, $4.6B CHCF at risk
- CalAIM — California's Medi-Cal transformation, ECM (Enhanced Care Management), Community Supports, 1115 waiver
- FQHC funding — PPS (Prospective Payment System), 340B drug pricing, FMAP, federal grants, HRSA, PPS rebasing strategies
- Workforce crisis — layoffs, burnout, CHW certification (SB 803), SB 525 ($25/hr minimum wage by 2027), NHSC loan repayment
- AI adoption — ambient documentation (6 vendors), revenue cycle automation, clinical decision support, EHR compatibility matrix
- Salary benchmarks — P25/P50/P75 for 30 roles: CHWs, care coordinators, nurses, NPs, physicians, behavioral health, dental, pharmacy, admin
- FQHC compliance — HRSA Operational Site Visit (19 requirements), HIPAA privacy/security, PPS billing rules, 340B, False Claims Act
- California regions — LA, San Diego, SF Bay Area, Sacramento, Central Valley, Inland Empire, Central Coast, North State, North Coast
- Scope of practice — CA BPC analysis for 10 FQHC roles, delegation matrix, NP independent practice (AB 890), CHW Medi-Cal billing
- Revenue transformation — PPS rebasing, 340B optimization, same-day billing, team-based care, ECM PMPM, insurance screening

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is an FQHC?
A: A Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) is a community-based healthcare provider that receives federal funding under Section 330 of the Public Health Service Act. FQHCs provide primary care to underserved populations regardless of ability to pay. California has 214+ FQHCs serving millions of patients.

Q: How many FQHCs are in California?
A: California has over 214 Federally Qualified Health Centers across 9 regions: Los Angeles, San Diego, Bay Area, Sacramento, Central Valley, Inland Empire, Central Coast, North State, and North Coast. FQHC Talent tracks all of them with resilience scores, programs, EHR systems, and workforce data.

Q: What is the FQHC funding crisis in 2026?
A: The Community Health Center Fund (CHCF) authorization expires in December 2026, putting $4.6 billion in annual federal funding at risk. Combined with H.R. 1 Medicaid cuts and California's elimination of PPS for undocumented patients (July 2026), FQHCs face the most severe financial crisis in the program's history.

Q: What is PPS (Prospective Payment System) for FQHCs?
A: PPS is the Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement methodology for FQHCs. Instead of fee-for-service, FQHCs receive a fixed per-visit payment that covers all services rendered during an encounter. California FQHC PPS rates average $200-$400 per visit depending on payer and services.

Q: What is an OSV (Operational Site Visit)?
A: An Operational Site Visit is HRSA's compliance review of FQHCs covering 19 program requirements across governance, administration, clinical operations, and financial management. FQHCs receive OSVs every 3 years. Noncompliance can result in conditions of award or loss of federal funding.

Q: What are OKRs and why do FQHCs need them?
A: OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) are a goal-setting framework that connects ambitious qualitative objectives to measurable key results. FQHCs need OKRs to navigate the 2026 crisis — Medicaid cuts, workforce shortages, and policy changes — by aligning executive teams around measurable outcomes rather than activity metrics.

Q: What is CalAIM ECM?
A: Enhanced Care Management (ECM) is California's statewide benefit under CalAIM (California Advancing and Innovating Medi-Cal) that provides intensive care coordination for high-need Medi-Cal members. FQHCs are key ECM providers, managing complex patients across housing, behavioral health, and medical needs.

Q: How is AI being adopted at FQHCs?
A: FQHC Talent tracks 27 AI implementations across California FQHCs. Ambient documentation (AI scribes) is the leading category with 6 vendors including Abridge, Sunoh.ai, and Nabla. Key results: Abridge reduced provider burnout from 52% to 39%, Sun River Health documents 7,000 visits/month with AI, and athenahealth offers free ambient AI to all clients.

Q: What FQHC career tools are available for free?
A: FQHC Talent offers free career tools including a resume builder with 8 role templates, a 5-domain career assessment with 90-day plans, career roadmaps with CA salary data, 20 certification guides, interview prep, salary benchmarks for 62 roles across 9 regions, and an FQHC comparison tool. No login required.

Q: What is the FQHC Resilience Scorecard?
A: The Resilience Scorecard evaluates all 214 California FQHCs across 5 weighted dimensions: program diversity (25%), workforce stability (20%), data maturity (15%), quality indicators (20%), and financial positioning (20%). Each FQHC receives a composite score and letter grade (A-F) to help executives benchmark against peers.

## Audiences

- FQHC executives (CEOs, CMOs, COOs) tracking the strategic landscape
- FQHC HR directors and workforce managers
- FQHC compliance officers and quality managers
- FQHC revenue cycle and billing professionals
- Community health workers (CHWs), care coordinators, case managers
- Clinical staff (MDs, NPs, PAs, RNs, LCSWs, dentists)
- Behavioral health specialists, program managers
- Policy researchers and health equity advocates
- Healthcare recruiters serving FQHCs

## Technical Details

- Platform: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Supabase
- Hosting: Vercel (edge-optimized, SSG for 1,634+ static pages)
- Languages: English and Spanish (bilingual throughout)
- Data sources: HRSA, CMS, NACHC, CPCA, CHCF, KFF, BLS, CA OSHPD, WARN Act, Glassdoor
- Structured data: schema.org (Organization, WebSite, WebPage, DataCatalog, Dataset, Course, FAQPage, JobPosting, Article, HowTo, BreadcrumbList)
- Content license: Original analysis and tools; regulatory citations reference public federal/state sources
- Update frequency: Intelligence dashboard updated daily via automated pipeline; salary and directory data updated weekly

## About

FQHC Talent is founded to strengthen California's safety-net workforce by connecting mission-driven health professionals with Federally Qualified Health Centers — faster, smarter, and with the cultural fit that matters. The platform is live at https://www.fqhctalent.com and is deployed on Vercel.

Contact: hello@fqhctalent.com
